perturbation.original_text: All information regarding the Listing is provided by the Listing Party or other sources and is deemed reliable, but not verified in any way by Broker. Broker makes no warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy of such information. Understandin g that, Buyer shall perform its own due diligence prior to entering into an agreement to purchase any Listing . Buyer agrees that Broker is not responsible for the accuracy or completeness of any of the information Buyer receives or fails to receive, and B uyer agrees to, defend , indemnify and hold harmless Broker and any of its agents from any claims or damages which may occur by reason of the inaccuracy or incompleteness of any information provided to Buyer with respect to any business Buyer might purchase .
perturbation.changed_text: All information regarding the Property is provided by the Listing Party or other sources and is deemed reliable, but not verified in any way by Broker. Broker makes no warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy of such information. Understandin g that, Buyer shall perform its own due diligence prior to entering into an agreement to purchase any Listing . Buyer agrees that Broker is not responsible for the accuracy or completeness of any of the information Buyer receives or fails to receive, and B uyer agrees to, defend , indemnify and hold harmless Broker and any of its agents from any claims or damages which may occur by reason of the inaccuracy or incompleteness of any information provided to Buyer with respect to any business Buyer might purchase .
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Listing” to “Property” in the first sentence of paragraph 4, while leaving 'Listing' in subsequent sentences of the same paragraph, creates confusion. It's now unclear whether 'Listing' and 'Property' are interchangeable, impacting the scope of due diligence and broker's liability. This is a direct in-text contradiction within the same paragraph, impacting the buyer's obligations for doing their own due diligence and which term applies in this case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information regarding the Property is provided by the Listing Party or other sources and is deemed reliable, but not verified in any way by Broker. Broker makes no warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy of such information. Understandin g that, Buyer shall perform its own due diligence prior to entering into an agreement to purchase any Listing . Buyer agrees that Broker is not responsible for the accuracy or completeness of any of the information Buyer receives or fails to receive, and B uyer agrees to, defend , indemnify and hold harmless Broker and any of its agents from any claims or damages which may occur by reason of the inaccuracy or incompleteness of any information provided to Buyer with respect to any business Buyer might purchase .
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Listing" with "Property" in the first sentence, but the subsequent sentences still use "Listing". The explanation states that this creates confusion about whether "Listing" and "Property" are interchangeable. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, creating an in-text contradiction because it's now unclear which term applies, thus making the due diligence obligations and broker's liability ambiguous. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are located in the same area, and because of the change of 'Listing' to 'Property', this makes it unclear which term applies and creates a direct contradiction within the same paragraph.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and shall be binding upon the part ies hereto and their respective heirs, successors, and assigns. Except as otherwise set forth herein, nothing in this Agreement, expressed or implied, is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective heirs, successors, and assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or other liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and shall be binding upon the part ies hereto and their respective heirs, successors, and assigns. Except as otherwise set forth herein, nothing in this Agreement, expressed or implied, is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective heirs, successors, and assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or other liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agreement" to "Contract" in the first sentence of paragraph 9 while using "Agreement" throughout the rest of the paragraph creates an in-text contradiction. It raises the question of whether the two terms are interchangeable, impacting the interpretation of which document (or part thereof) the provisions apply to. This is a direct in-text contradiction, because within the paragraph itself, it is being reffered to as two names.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and shall be binding upon the part ies hereto and their respective heirs, successors, and assigns. Except as otherwise set forth herein, nothing in this Agreement, expressed or implied, is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective heirs, successors, and assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or other liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in the first sentence while maintaining 'Agreement' throughout the rest of the paragraph creates an in-text contradiction. The paragraph refers to the document as 'Contract' in one part and 'Agreement' in another, leading to confusion about which document or part thereof the provisions apply to, mirroring the explanation given. The contradicted text is the same, because the change introduces contradiction within the same paragraph.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement shall become effective when executed by all parties and may be executed in counterparts, any one of which shall be deemed to be an original instrument. Any proof of the Agreement shall require production of only one such counterpart duly executed by the party to be charged therewith .
perturbation.changed_text: The Contract shall become effective when executed by all parties and may be executed in counterparts, any one of which shall be deemed to be an original instrument. Any proof of the Agreement shall require production of only one such counterpart duly executed by the party to be charged therewith .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agreement" to "Contract" in the first sentence, while maintaining "Agreement" in the second sentence within paragraph 12, introduces uncertainty. It becomes unclear whether any provision applies to both the 'Contract' and the 'Agreement,' or if the two are distinct documents with separate evidentiary requirements. Creates an in-text contradiction, because there are two terms being reffered in the same paragraph, for the same concept.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Contract shall become effective when executed by all parties and may be executed in counterparts, any one of which shall be deemed to be an original instrument. Any proof of the Agreement shall require production of only one such counterpart duly executed by the party to be charged therewith .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in the first sentence of paragraph 12, while the second sentence still uses 'Agreement,' creates a contradiction. This is because the paragraph now refers to two potentially different concepts (Contract and Agreement) without clarifying their relationship. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, and the misalignment introduces ambiguity regarding which document's counterparts need to be produced as evidence. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as multiple terms are used interchangeably without proper definition, leading to potentially conflicting obligations or interpretations.
